chunks.text: Lawrence A. Rowe is Professor Emeritus at UC Berkeley. He retired in June 2003 after twenty-seven years to pursue streaming media software projects, consulting with multimedia research labs, and investing in startups. He was the founding director of the Berkeley Multimedia Research Center (BMRC), created in 1995 to explore multimedia technology applications in education and research. BMRC taught multimedia authoring classes, operated authoring studios and collaboration rooms, and provided technical support. It closed in 2002 due to lack of funding.

Professor Rowe headed the research group that produced the Berkeley MPEG-1 Tools, the Berkeley Multimedia, Interfaces, and Graphics (MIG) Seminar Internet webcast, and the Open Mash Streaming Media Toolkit. He was responsible for developing and deploying the Berkeley lecture webcasting system. Earlier in his career, he worked on database application development tools and technologies commercialized by Ingres Corporation.

He received a BA in Mathematics and a PhD in Information and Computer Science from UC Irvine in 1970 and 1976, respectively. He is a Fellow of the ACM, past chair of the ACM Special Interest Group on Multimedia, and served on many governmental advisory committees. He has consulted and served on Technical Advisory Boards for numerous companies.

Professor Rowe co-founded several companies including Ingres Corporation, NCast Corporation, and Orinda Software, Inc. He served on the Board of Directors of Ingres Corporation, NCast Corporation, and Siemens Technology-to-Business. His selected honors include the ACM SIGMOD Systems Award (2015), ACM SIGMM Technical Achievement Award (2009), ACM Fellow (1997), and Diane S. McEntyre Award for Excellence in Teaching (1995).
